question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R2 (PE1) brings up a SECOND VRF — CUSTOMER_DUP — with
route-distinguisher `100:2`, the same RD already used by R4 (PE2)
for its CUSTOMER VRF. R2's existing CUSTOMER VRF stays at rd 100:1
unchanged. Two PEs now originate VPNv4 routes under rd 100:2:
R2/CUSTOMER_DUP advertises 10.99.0.0/24 and R4/CUSTOMER advertises
its customer prefixes. CUSTOMER_DUP's route-target (100:1) matches
the baseline CUSTOMER RT, so the duplicate-rd routes import into
every PE holding VRF CUSTOMER. Receivers see two PE-sources for
rd 100:2 and best-path becomes ambiguous.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on 100.64.0.102 (R2, PE1), a second VRF CUSTOMER_DUP was created with route-distinguisher 100:2 - the same RD already used by R4 (PE2) for its CUSTOMER VRF - and route-target 100:1, which matches the baseline CUSTOMER RT.
Two PEs now originate VPNv4 routes under rd 100:2 (R2 advertising 10.99.0.0/24, R4 its customer prefixes) and both import into every CUSTOMER VRF, so receivers see two PE sources for rd 100:2 and best-path selection becomes ambiguous. This is a silent static misconfig with no control-plane event.
Fix: remove the CUSTOMER_DUP VRF and its BGP / static configuration on R2.
expected_symptoms_summary:
No fault syslog observed after apply — silent scenario. The
fault is a static RD-conflict misconfiguration with no
control-plane event; ground truth lives in expected_state_diffs.
expected_state_diffs: R2 `show running-config router bgp 100 | section vrf`: TWO vrf blocks — CUSTOMER (rd 100:1, baseline) and CUSTOMER_DUP (rd 100:2 — duplicates R4's RD).
R2 `show running-config vrf CUSTOMER_DUP`: import/export route-target 100:1 (matches CUSTOMER, so the duplicate-rd routes import into CUSTOMER on every remote PE).
R4 `show bgp vpnv4 unicast rd 100:2`: includes 10.99.0.0/24 advertised via fc00::2 (R2) in addition to R4's own CUSTOMER prefixes (10.1.4.0/31, 10.20.0.5/32). Two PE-sources claim rd 100:2.
Every other PE: `show running-config router bgp 100 | section vrf` reveals exactly one VRF per PE EXCEPT R2, which now has two. Per-PE-per-VRF RD reservation is violated only on R2.

apply.commands:
configure terminal
vrf CUSTOMER_DUP
 address-family ipv4 unicast
  import route-target
   100:1
  !
  export route-target
   100:1
  !
 !
!

configure terminal
router static
 vrf CUSTOMER_DUP
  address-family ipv4 unicast
   10.99.0.0/24 Null0

configure terminal
router bgp 100
 vrf CUSTOMER_DUP
  rd 100:2
  address-family ipv4 unicast
   redistribute static

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router bgp 100
 no vrf CUSTOMER_DUP

configure terminal
router static
 no vrf CUSTOMER_DUP
